verses.text: Achish, sure of David's loyalty, tells him to march out with the Philistines against Israel, and David gives a slippery answer. His compromise has cornered him: he is expected to fight his own people. Sin's tangled web tightens until escape seems impossible.

Achish, sure of David's loyalty, tells him to march out with the Philistines against Israel, and David gives a slippery answer. His compromise has cornered him: he is expected to fight his own people. Sin's tangled web tightens until escape seems impossible.

Samuel is dead, and Saul — who had himself banished the mediums — faces a vast Philistine army in terror. He asks God, but heaven is silent: no dream, no Urim, no prophet. The man who long ignored God now cannot find Him. Long-refused mercy may at last fall quiet.

Samuel is dead, and Saul — who had himself banished the mediums — faces a vast Philistine army in terror. He asks God, but heaven is silent: no dream, no Urim, no prophet. The man who long ignored God now cannot find Him. Long-refused mercy may at last fall quiet.

Desperate, Saul seeks out the very witch he had outlawed, disguising himself to consult the dead. The king who once stood among the prophets now creeps by night to a medium. A heart cut off from God will turn anywhere for guidance, even to the darkness it once condemned.

The woman, asked to bring up Samuel, cries out in fright and realizes her visitor is the king. Whatever the means, God permits Samuel to appear — and Saul bows to the ground. The dead prophet brings no comfort, only the dread of facing the word Saul had spurned in life.

Samuel's verdict is final: 'the LORD is departed from thee, and is become thine enemy... to morrow shalt thou and thy sons be with me.' The judgment long pronounced now lands. Years of disobedience meet their reckoning; the man who would not hear God in life hears only doom.

Saul falls full length to the ground, drained of strength, having eaten nothing all day. The proud king is utterly broken. The woman, with rough kindness, urges him to eat. There is a hollowness at the end of a life that pushed God away — strength gone, hope gone, only dread left.

Saul falls full length to the ground, drained of strength, having eaten nothing all day. The proud king is utterly broken. The woman, with rough kindness, urges him to eat. There is a hollowness at the end of a life that pushed God away — strength gone, hope gone, only dread left.

Saul falls full length to the ground, drained of strength, having eaten nothing all day. The proud king is utterly broken. The woman, with rough kindness, urges him to eat. There is a hollowness at the end of a life that pushed God away — strength gone, hope gone, only dread left.

At first he refuses, but his servants and the woman press him, and Saul eats and rises to go into the night. He returns to a battle he knows he will lose, to a death already foretold. It is a desolate scene — a king walking knowingly toward his doom, with God departed.
